title: orchis collina subsp. chlorotica
intro: Orchis collina subsp. chlorotica, also known as the green-flowered orchid, is a subspecies of the orchid plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Orchis collina subsp. chlorotica include its ability to treat various conditions such as urinary tract infections, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
